presentation.latex: attributed graph G = (A, X) with n nodes V and m edges E that is represented by an adjacency matrix A = {0, 1}n×n and a node feature matrix X ∈ Rn×d. Each node has a label yi ∈ {1, . . . C}. Given the labels of training nodes, the task is to infer the labels of the remaining nodes. presentation.unicode_plain: attributed graph G = (A, X) with n nodes V and m edges E that is represented by an adjacency matrix A = {0, 1}n×n and a node feature matrix X ∈ Rn×d. Each node has a label yi ∈ {1, . . . C}. Given the labels of training nodes, the task is to infer the labels of the remaining nodes. Lastly, we focus on homophilic graphs, i.e. edges are predominantly present between nodes of the same class. 1We provide our code at cs.cit.tum.de/daml/gebm/

evidence.verbatim_snippet: attributed graph G = (A, X) with n nodes V and m edges E that is represented by an adjacency matrix A = {0, 1}n×n and a node feature matrix X ∈ Rn×d. Each node has a label yi ∈ {1, . . . C}. Given the labels of training nodes, the task is to infer the labels of the remaining nodes. Lastly, we focus on homophilic graphs, i.e. edges are predominantly present between nodes of the same class. 1We provide our code at cs.cit.tum.de/daml/gebm/

evidence.verbatim_snippet: matrix A = {0, 1}n×n and a node feature matrix X ∈ Rn×d. Each node has a label yi ∈ {1, . . . C}. Given the labels of training nodes, the task is to infer the labels of the remaining nodes. Lastly, we focus on homophilic graphs, i.e. edges are predominantly present between nodes of the same class. 1We provide our code at cs.cit.tum.de/daml/gebm/ 2
